title: Locate the Andromeda Galaxy
description: Use a planisphere star chart, a red flashlight and a basic telescope to locate our neighboring galaxy while preserving night vision.

dialogue.text: Nova here. Choose a dark, open spot away from traffic, check the forecast and let your eyes adjust for 15 minutes. Keep screens dim, secure your basic telescope and use a planisphere star chart with a red flashlight to preserve night vision. How do you want to run tonight's hunt?

options.text: I'll stage the safe observing site first.
Clouds and porch lights are rough—what's the fallback plan?

dialogue.text: Good call. Confirm a stable tripod footprint, keep your red flashlight pointed down, and record your target area in the mission logbook before you sweep.
If cloud cover blocks the view, pause and reset. Shift to a darker edge, wait for a forecast gap, and never cross unsafe ground in the dark.

options.text: Rechecking landmarks while I wait for clearer sky.

dialogue.text: Shield the red flashlight to avoid glare and trace Cassiopeia's W on the chart. Sweep the basic telescope at low magnification toward the faint smudge. Keep footing sure and never aim near the Sun or aircraft.

options.text: I lost the target in haze or glare.
Galaxy in sight and logged with location notes.

dialogue.text: Recovery loop: reduce magnification, re-center Cassiopeia, and wait two more minutes for full dark adaptation. If dew forms, cap and wipe optics before retrying.

options.text: I'll reset and verify alignment before another sweep.

dialogue.text: Nice work! Log the position for next time, cap the telescope, switch off the flashlight and pack your gear before leaving the site.
